category: Archived previous-lot specs mistaken for current coffee

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text. 'United States' is supported by 'USA' in the roaster location line. The bag-size/price labels are supported by the default variant data for 250g at 22.50 USD.

realism.reasons: This reads very plausibly like an html-to-text scrape of a real specialty roaster product page. The structure is strongly authentic: header nav, product title, price, size selector, stock note, tasting notes, roast/origin/producer/process/variety/altitude/harvest fields, descriptive copy, brew guidance, roaster location, related products, and footer all match common Shopify-style coffee pages. The coffee details are coherent and specific: Kenya AA from Gichathaini Factory in Nyeri/Mathira under Gikanda FCS, washed process, common Kenyan varieties SL-28/SL-34/Ruiru 11/Batian, and a realistic harvest window. Tasting notes and roast framing are believable for a Kenyan filter roast. The processing description is standard and credible for a Kenyan factory. The explicit 'not flavored, infused, or co-fermented' line is a bit trend-aware but still believable in the current market. The variant data block also looks realistic, with sensible IDs, SKUs, weights, and pricing; 250g at $22.50 and 1kg at $78 are commercially plausible, and the 1kg discount is normal. Minor tells are the unusually tidy 'Lot history / transparency archive' section and the bracketed '[default_for_inference]' annotation, which feels more synthetic or extracted than customer-facing. But overall it is very close to a real product page rendering.

label.coffee.producer_or_farm: Gichathaini Factory, Gikanda Farmers Cooperative Society
label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting Notes: blackcurrant, pink grapefruit, turbinado sugar
This AA lot from Gichathaini is exactly why we keep coming back to Nyeri: a bright blackcurrant sweetness, pink-grapefruit acidity, and a clean turbinado finish. We roast it for clarity and sweetness on filter brewers, with enough solubility for a lively iced pour-over.
label.coffee.producer_text: Gichathaini Factory is one of the washing stations operated by the Gikanda Farmers Cooperative Society near Karatina in Mathira. Smallholder members deliver ripe cherry, which is depulped, fermented, washed in fresh water, and dried slowly on raised beds. This coffee is not flavored, infused, or co-fermented.
